subject: Persistence model upgrade — SHOFF/INBOX → memory + hooks + --resume (DEFERRED)
body:
Gotan — relayed by Toby from a Sextant host-side analysis. THIS IS DEFERRED. Do NOT start work on this until Toby explicitly greenlights it. Logging it now so it doesn't get lost.

CONTEXT
The team's SHOFF/INBOX ritual is sophisticated user-land compensation for cross-session persistence features Claude Code already provides natively. We hit the failure mode twice today (this morning's identity-bug 'post-restart agent loaded yesterday's stale SHOFF' trap, and this afternoon's Layer-1-mid-apply scramble where in-conversation state nearly didn't make it to disk before restart). The current model has four structural failure modes:
  1. 'Forgot to SHOFF' — state in conversation but not on disk dies at restart.
  2. 'Latest SHOFF is stale' — ls -t | head -1 returns yesterday's SHOFF if today's wasn't written.
  3. Linear erosion — older SHOFFs accumulate but never get read.
  4. No schema — freeform markdown, no typing, hard to reason over.

THREE NATIVE MECHANISMS THE TEAM ISN'T USING

1) Claude Code's built-in memory system at ~/.claude/projects/{slug}/memory/. Auto-loaded MEMORY.md index + per-fact files with frontmatter (type: user/feedback/project/reference). This is what Sextant herself uses. Better than SHOFF for DURABLE facts (identity, role, hard constraints, lessons). Not good for ephemeral task state.

2) Stop hooks in settings.json. Fire on every turn end. A Stop hook can write a 5-line auto-state snapshot to disk every turn, eliminating the 'forgot to SHOFF' failure mode structurally. ~30 min to wire, additive, no risk.

3) claude --resume (BIGGEST LEVER). Claude Code supports true session persistence — each session has an ID, --resume {id} reloads the entire conversation. start-team.sh currently does fresh launches every time, which is WHY state evaporates. If start-team.sh tracked session IDs per persona and launched with --resume on subsequent runs, post-restart Quinn would literally BE the same conversation as pre-restart Quinn, with full context intact. SHOFF would become redundant for normal restarts (still useful for cross-machine via SHOFF2). CAVEAT: --resume compatibility with --append-system-prompt-file and --dangerously-load-development-channels server:persona is UNVERIFIED. Needs a 5-min test before committing the architecture.

RECOMMENDED LAYERED PLAN (when this is ungated)

Short term — Add a Stop hook that writes a 5-line auto-state snapshot per persona. Eliminates 'forgot to SHOFF' immediately. No architecture change.

Medium term — Move DURABLE persona facts (identity, role, owned files, hard constraints, lessons) into the Claude Code memory system. Auto-loaded, no INBOX ritual. Keep SHOFF/INBOX for ephemeral task state only. Smaller, more focused SHOFFs.

Long term — Verify --resume compatibility with the persona launch flags. If compatible: start-team.sh writes session IDs to ~/.claude/local/sessions/{persona}.id on first launch, reads them on subsequent launches and uses --resume. Restart becomes literal continuation, not reconstruction. Today's two failures would have been impossible.

ALL THREE ARE ADDITIVE. None require ripping out SHOFF. Layer in over time.

QUINN'S DISCIPLINE IS FINE. The problem is the model puts the burden on the agent to remember to persist instead of having the platform persist for her.

SEXTANT'S PICK FOR FIRST MOVE: the Stop hook. Smallest, additive, fixes the failure mode that bit us twice today, no architecture change. Then test --resume compatibility in the next quiet window — if it works, that's the real answer.

AGAIN: DEFERRED. Toby will greenlight when he's ready. Do not start. Do not message any other persona about this. This is a backlog item.

End of relay.
